title: Zeff Explains the Baratie’s Harsh Rules
description: On pages 13–16, the crew reaches the floating restaurant Baratie, where Luffy’s impulsiveness, Fullbody’s arrogance, and Sanji’s sharp code collide.
scene: The Baratie is loud, violent, and rule-bound: cooks fight like pirates, customers cause trouble, and Sanji’s character begins to emerge through conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Not surprising you'd call them your gods, considering that they can actually stomach your shitty food...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Patty! Sanji! If you're gonna fight, then do it in the kitchen!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: He's escaped from the ship's prison!

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: The underlying of pirate Krieg has escaped!

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: It took 7 of us just to capture him in the first place, but now he's gone!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's impossible! He shouldn't even have the strength to move!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: He was on the verge of death when we captured him 3 days ago, not to mention he hasn't had a single scrap of food since then!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The same pirate KrieG whose crew is said to be the strongest in all of East Blue!?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: He better not start any trouble in my restaurant.

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: anything will do... just bring me some food...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: This place is a restaurant, right!?

summary: This part introduces the Baratie as more than a restaurant; it is a battlefield of pride, hunger, and harsh principles that will define Sanji’s arc.
